member.name: T:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.BluetoothSmart
member.summary: A BluetoothSmart module for Microsoft .NET Gadgeteer

member.name: T:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial
member.summary: Extends the .NET Micro Framework SerialPort Class with additional methods from the Full .NET Framework SerialPort Class as well as other useful methods.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified string to the serial port.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.WriteLine(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified string and the NewLine value to the output buffer.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.ReadExistingBinary
member.summary: Reads all immediately available bytes, as binary data, in both the stream and the input buffer of the SerialPort object.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.ReadExisting
member.summary: Reads all immediately available bytes, based on the encoding, in both the stream and the input buffer of the SerialPort object.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.Open
member.summary: Opens a new serial port connection.

member.name: F:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial._Remainder
member.summary: Stores any incomplete message that hasn't yet been terminated with a delimiter. This will be concatenated with new data from the next DataReceived event to (hopefully) form a complete message. This property is only populated after the Deserialize() method has been called.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.Deserialize(System.String)
member.summary: Splits data from a serial buffer into separate messages, provided that each message is delimited by one or more end-of-line character(s).
member.param: Character sequence that terminates a message line. Default is "\r\n".
member.returns:
An array of strings whose items correspond to individual messages, without the delimiters. Only complete, properly terminated messages are included. Incomplete message fragments are saved to be appended to the next received data.
If no complete messages are found in the serial buffer, the output array will be empty with Length = 0.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.SplitString(System.String,System.String@,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Splits a stream into separate lines, given a delimiter.
member.param:
The string that will be deserialized.
Example: Assume a device transmits serial messages, and each message is separated by 
 (carriage return + line feed).
For illustration, picture the following output from such a device: First message.
 Second message.
 Third message.
 Fourth message.

Once a SerialPort object receives the first bytes, the DataReceived event will be fired, and the interrupt handler may read a string from the serial buffer like so: "First message.
Second message.
Third message.
Fourth me"
The message above has been cut off to simulate the DataReceived event being fired before the sender has finished transmitting all messages (the "ssage.
" characters have not yet traveled down the wire, so to speak). At the moment the DataReceived event is fired, the interrupt handler only has access to the (truncated) input message above.
In this example, the string from the serial buffer will be the input to this method.

Any incomplete messages that have not yet been properly terminated will be returned via this output parameter. In the above example, this parameter will return "Fourth me". Ideally, this output parameter will be appended to the next transmission to reconstruct the next complete message.
A string specifying the delimiter between messages. If omitted, this defaults to "\r\n" (carriage return + line feed).

Determines whether each item in the output array will include the specified delimiter. If True, the delimiter will be included at the end of each string in the output array. If False (default), the delimiter will be excluded from the output strings.
member.returns:
string[] Every item in this string array will be an individual, complete message. The first element in the array corresponds to the first message, and so forth. The length of the array will be equal to the number of complete messages extracted from the input string.
From the above example, if includeDelimiterInOutput == True, this output will be: output[0] = "First message.
" output[1] = "Second message.
" output[2] = "Third message.
"
If no complete messages have been received, the output array will be empty with Length = 0.

member.name: M:Gadgeteer.Modules.IngenuityMicro.SimpleSerial.ScrubStringArray(System.String[],System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Removes items in an input array that are equal to a specified string.
member.param: String whose occurrences will be removed if an item consists of it. Default: string.Empty.

Delimiter that will be appended to the end of each element in the output array. Default: \r\n (carriage return + line feed). To omit delimiters from the end of each message, set this parameter to string.Empty.
member.returns: String array containing only desired strings. The length of this output will likely be shorter than the input array.
